items.minimax_notes: Reasoning identified all 4 answers correctly (312/128 KB, 05:11 UTC at 8 pods/min, the two March action items, 84ms->9.1s ratio ~108x). But finish_reason=length truncated the FINAL block partway through the Q3 table, so Q4 was never delivered in the user-visible answer. No hallucinations.
items.dsv4_notes: All 4 questions answered concisely and correctly in 242 tokens. Q4 chose the IMPACT '15 s' figure (ratio 178.57) which the rubric explicitly accepts. Owners and dates on Q3 correct. No hallucinations.
items.key_diff: MiniMax burned 1500 tokens on scratch reasoning and got truncated; DSV4 went straight to the FINAL block and delivered the full answer set.

items.minimax_notes: Long scratchpad reasoning correctly derived Q1 (250 ms / Section 5), Q2 (110,080 GB), and Q3 (AV-TEN-3318 / 2025-11-22 / 7-way partition). Then finish_reason=length truncated mid-Q4 listing only OQ-1 and the first letter of OQ-2. The FINAL block is essentially empty -- only '[FINAL]' delivered, no formatted answers reach the user.
items.dsv4_notes: All 4 correct in 327 tokens. Q2 arithmetic 85.8 GB x 256 x 5 = 109,568 GB (rubric explicitly accepts close arithmetic vs the document's 86 GB rounding). Q4 listed all three OQs verbatim and correctly named D. Trinh as the pending response on OQ-2.
items.key_diff: MiniMax answered everything in its scratchpad but emitted no formatted FINAL; DSV4 delivered a clean, complete formatted answer.

items.minimax_notes: All 4 correct. Q1: 5.829 vs 5.812, diff 0.017. Q2: gains begin at 32, substantial at 64+. Q3: width 65536->8192, divergence at 110B tokens, 3 of 5 seeds. Q4: all three limitations verbatim, error-feedback buffer (1.0x param size) identified as the memory one. No hallucinations.
items.dsv4_notes: All 4 correct, concise. Q3 adds a brief attribution about 'increased collision rate causing systematic bias' which is a plausible inference but not strictly quoted from the document; mild but not a hallucination of facts. Q4 verbatim and memory limitation correctly flagged.
items.key_diff: Both nail all four. MiniMax is more decorated with markdown tables; DSV4 is half the length. No factual difference.

items.minimax_notes: All 4 correct. 250 MB + content-type rejection; 100% sampling for SSN/CREDIT_CARD/MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER vs 1% otherwise; oversubscription 13,920/1,100=12.65x with bimodal 4-7x size driver; IQ-3 is P2.
items.dsv4_notes: All 4 correct in 233 tokens. Same numbers, same P2 identification, same bimodal-distribution rationale. Clean and complete.
items.key_diff: Identical correctness; DSV4 uses ~1/4 the tokens for the same answer quality.

items.minimax_notes: Scratchpad reasoning correct on all 4 (10^25 FLOP + 72hr; the 3 UK elements with 30-day/6-year/12-20M GBP; >10^26 training and >3x10^25 fine-tuning, narrowed to critical harms; model-eval+red-team logs with UK 6yr / US 18mo / EU Article 16 event-level). But finish_reason=length truncated FINAL mid-Q3 ('mass casualty ('). Q4 never delivered in FINAL.
items.dsv4_notes: All 4 questions answered cleanly in 492 tokens. Hit every rubric element including the three numeric UK reserve figure (12-20M GBP), the critical-harms narrowing, and the three-jurisdiction retention split (UK 6yr / US 18mo / EU Article 16 event-level).
items.key_diff: MiniMax again burned its 1500-token budget on reasoning and never finished the user-visible Q3/Q4; DSV4's terse style fit comfortably in budget.

items.minimax_notes: All 4 correct. Three changes with both before/after values (128->192 GB, 900 GB/s->1.3 TB/s, 580 GB/s->1.0 TB/s). Q2 computed both speedups (3.25x and 1.94x) and reconciled with document's 3.2x/1.9x. Q3: >850 GB/s threshold, drops to 760 GB/s. Q4: batch >24, intra-node bandwidth saturates and all-to-all amortizes.
items.dsv4_notes: All 4 correct in 297 tokens. Hit every rubric number. Used the document's 3.2x/1.9x rounded speedups rather than computing 3.25x/1.94x from first principles; rubric accepts either form.
items.key_diff: Identical correctness; DSV4 is one-fifth the length. MiniMax shows its arithmetic, DSV4 just states the conclusion.

summary.overall_takeaway: Terseness emphatically helped DSV4, did not hurt it. The hot question reverses: MiniMax's verbose chain-of-thought ate its 1500-token budget on E1, E2, and E5, causing finish_reason=length truncation that left the FINAL block incomplete or empty even though the scratchpad reasoning had reached correct answers. DSV4 skipped the visible reasoning and went straight to formatted [FINAL] answers in 233-492 tokens, never once missing a question. On the three items where MiniMax did fit (E3, E4, E6) the two models tied at 10/10. So on long-context recall, DSV4's concise style was a pure asset here -- it was not too terse to cover 4-part questions, and it avoided the truncation tax MiniMax paid on half the items. Recommendation: cap MiniMax reasoning tokens or raise max_tokens for this category.
